name: Pancakes
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix all the dry ingredients together.
2. Beat egg yolk and bacon grease in a separate bowl.
3. Add buttermilk to the egg mixture and stir until combined.
4. Gradually add the wet mixture to the flour mixture, folding until just combined.
5. Stir in beaten egg white.
6. Cook pancakes on a hot griddle or skillet.
7. Adjust amounts of ingredients as desired. The key to light pancakes is beating the egg white well and folding it into the batter.
8. If not using all the batter at once, store leftovers in the refrigerator. They can be kept for up to 2 days and make a nice sourdough starter if left out overnight.
9. Serve with blackberry jam instead of syrup. Butter is optional.
This recipe provides guidelines only; feel free to adjust ingredient quantities as preferred. The best pancakes come from beating egg whites well before folding them into the batter.
